sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the light green box (N) moved up! It was at the bottom of the gray box with light green, and now it's moved up one row. So the arrow keys control the light green box!
Let me compare the before and after more carefully to understand what moved.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what changed
prev_ascii = previous_frame.ascii.split('\n')
curr_ascii = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("Changes (rows around the light green box):")
for i in range(38, 44):
    prev = prev_ascii[i][7:28]
    curr = curr_ascii[i][7:28]
    marker = " <-- CHANGED" if prev != curr else ""
    print(f"  row {i}: prev={prev}")
    print(f"           curr={curr}{marker}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The light green box (NN) moved up by 2 rows! It was at rows 40-41 and is now at rows 38-39. So the UP arrow moves the light green box upward within its gray container.
Now I understand the game better:
- Arrow keys move the light green box (or possibly other colored elements)
- Mouse clicks on the right containers toggle colored objects on the left
The goal might be to move the light green box to a specific position, or to move it to match some target.
Let me explore more by pressing UP again to see if the light green box can move further, and also try other directions.
